prompt: Actually, I think it's not bad at all. It means that you use all your resources to get better. I chose the same way of studying, I try to get high marks and so I almost don't have any free time either. But I believe this is the right decition. However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. I can give you some tips how to get it. Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. For example, I like to do a lot of assignments on weekends, so each my day gets easier. But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. However, some people prefer to have fully free weekends. So you should try both and decide which option is better for you. Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. But very important thing: don't abuse this help. I hope my tips will be useful for you.

Return ONLY one CEFR label from: A2|B1|B2.
Do not output JSON.
Do not output explanation.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Actually, I think it's not bad at all. It means that you use all your resources to get better. I chose the same way of studying, I try to get high marks and so I almost don't have any free time either. But I believe this is the right decition. However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. I can give you some tips how to get it. Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. For example, I like to do a lot of assignments on weekends, so each my day gets easier. But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. However, some people prefer to have fully free weekends. So you should try both and decide which option is better for you. Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. But very important thing: don't abuse this help. I hope my tips will be useful for you.

I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. To sum up, you have to organise your days, set priorities.
